Florentino · Lightspeed · Allergy Procedure

Entering an Allergy Order

The exact steps to take an allergy order into Lightspeed — from the first hello at the table to the docket reaching the kitchen. Red circles point to exactly where to look on each screen.

★

The three places an allergy lives on the screen

Every allergy order touches the same three spots. Learn these and the steps below fall into place.

1
The table name (top). A flag the whole team sees — the allergy and/or "ANAPHYLACTIC" sits next to the table number.
2
The purple ALLERGY button. Adds an ALLERGY line where you enter each allergy against its seat number.
3
Each affected dish. Carries its own "No ___ – sub ___" substitution and seat, so it prints on the kitchen docket.
Start here — before the menu

Ask about allergies first

As you greet the table, ask if anyone has a food allergy — before you talk through the menu, so you can guide them to what they can and can't have. If the booking has an allergy noted in SevenRooms, confirm it out loud with the guest rather than assuming.

1

Flag it on the table name

Top of the screen, next to the table number — tag the order name so the flag is visible to everyone before a single dish is rung in.

1–2 allergies at the table
Allergy + seat

Write the allergy and the seat, e.g. SHELLFISH S1

3 or more allergies
"ALLERGY"

Too many to fit — just tag ALLERGY; the detail goes on the ALLERGY line (Step 2).

Any anaphylaxis
"ANAPHYLACTIC"

This always takes priority on the name tag. Then: log it & tell the manager.

Anaphylaxis → the name reads "[T333] ANAPHYLACTIC" (circle 1).
Several allergies → the name simply reads "[T333] ALLERGY".
Anaphylactic guest The moment a guest says they're anaphylactic: tag ANAPHYLACTIC on the name, log it on Lightspeed, and tell the manager — before taking the rest of the order.
2

Press ALLERGY & enter the allergies by seat

Tap the purple ALLERGY button (circle 2 in the overview). Open the line and enter each allergy against the seat it belongs to — plus an anaphylaxis flag if there is one.

1
Options tab — choose the allergen(s) and the seat here (e.g. ANAPHYLAXIS, SHELLFISH, Seat 1).
2
Note field (General tab) — for anything that needs free text the buttons don't cover.
3
The finished ALLERGY line appears in the order: the allergen(s) + seat, ready to send.
Enter every allergy with its seat One allergy or several — each goes in with the seat number it belongs to, so the kitchen knows exactly who it's for. For example: ANAPHYLAXIS, SHELLFISH, Seat 1.
3

Set up each dish: the "<Dish> MOD" section

When you add an affected dish, open its Options. Each dish has a "<dish name> MOD" group (e.g. FD MUSSEL MOD, FD PESCE MOD). Inside are CONTAINS buttons — showing which allergens the dish holds — and the substitutions you can make.

1
The dish's MOD group — here "FD SOUFFLE MOD". Open this to see the dish's allergen flags and its substitutions.
2
CONTAINS buttons — what the dish holds (alcohol, dairy, gluten, egg, nuts/pine nut). Check these against the guest's allergy.
3
Substitutions — the swaps you can apply, e.g. "No Nuts – No Pine Nuts", or "Halal / No Alcohol – sub Vanilla Gelato". Choose the seat too.
4
The result shows under the dish: "No Nuts – No Pine Nuts" + Seat 3 — exactly what prints for the kitchen.
The rule Under every dish a guest with an allergy is having, there must be a substitution, e.g. No seafood or shellfish – sub tomato. If no substitution is possible, the guest cannot have that dish — guide them to another.

Two more examples — a "No ___ – sub ___" substitution sits under each affected dish:

1
The dish's MOD group — here FD MUSSEL MOD (the CONTAINS buttons + substitutions live in here).
2
The substitution under the dish: "No seafood or shellfish – sub tomato" + Seat 1.
1
The dish's MOD group — here FD PESCE MOD.
2
The substitution under the dish: "No Shellfish – No Crab, No Bisque – Sub Gremolata" + Seat 1.
Split the quantity — never hide an allergy in a group line If a table orders 5 soufflés and one must be no pine nut, ring it as 4 × Soufflé on one line and 1 × Soufflé — No Pine Nut as its own line. The allergy plate must always stand on its own.

With several allergies at one table, each dish carries its own substitution and seat — so the kitchen sees exactly which plate is for whom:

1
No Gluten — Seat 1
2
No Dairy — Seat 2
3
No Nuts – No Pine Nuts — Seat 3 (its own line, separate from the others).
Not sure? If you're unsure whether a dish is safe or what it contains, check the Allergy Matrix (on Lightspeed, behind the bar, and at the back station). If the allergen isn't listed there, go and ask the chef — never guess.

The whole flow, in order

  1. Before the menu: ask about allergies / confirm any SevenRooms note with the guest.
  2. Tag the table name: 1–2 → allergy + seat · 3+ → "ALLERGY" · anaphylaxis → "ANAPHYLACTIC".
  3. Anaphylactic? Log it on Lightspeed and tell the manager — straight away.
  4. Press ALLERGY and enter every allergy against its seat number.
  5. Each dish via its "<Dish> MOD": add the substitution + seat. No substitution possible → the guest can't have it.
  6. Split quantities so each allergy plate is its own line (e.g. 4 normal + 1 No Pine Nut).
  7. Unsure? Allergy Matrix → if not listed, the chef. Never guess.
Entering an Allergy Order — Lightspeed. Internal training aid for Florentino floor staff. Screenshots are examples; always work from the live Allergy Matrix and current dish set-up.
